title: (type 8) heater mat
abstract: An aerospace surface heating apparatus and method for use on wing leading edges and the like. The apparatus comprising at least one electrically powered heating element located between opposing layers of a thermoplastic material. The thermoplastic material comprises an inorganic filler material blended with the thermoplastic.
claims: Claims 1. An aerospace surface heating apparatus, the apparatus comprising at least one electrically operable heating element located between opposing layers wherein the opposing layers are formed from a thermoplastic containing in excess of 20% by volume of an inorganic filler material. 2. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the thermoplastic contains between 20% and 30% by volume of an inorganic filler material. 3. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the thermoplastic is polyethertherketone (PEEK). 4. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the thermoplastic is polyaryletherketone (PAEK). 5. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the thermoplastic is polyetherketoneketone (PEKK). 6. A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the inorganic filler material is magnesium silicate. 7. A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the inorganic filler material is boron nitride. 8. A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the inorganic filler material is evenly distributed throughout the thermoplastic material. 9. A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the at least one electrically powered heating element is in the form of one or more tracks of a copper- containing conductor flame sprayed onto a thermoplastic substrate. 10. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 9 wherein the thermoplastic substrate comprises glass fibre. 1 1 . A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the apparatus comprises a first layer arranged for connection to an aircraft structure and a second layer arranged for connection to an erosion shield. 12. A heating apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the heating element is encapsulated in an intermediate layer between the opposing layers, and wherein the intermediate layer is in the form of a first and second thermoplastic layer on either side of the heating element, the thermoplastic layers comprising an inorganic filler material. 13. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 12, wherein the percentage of inorganic filler material contained in the intermediate layer in less than the percentage of inorganic filler material in the opposing layers. 14. A heating apparatus as claimed in claim 13 wherein the percentage of inorganic filler material contained in the intermediate layer is approximately 20%-30% and the percentage of inorganic filler material in the opposing layers is approximately 30%. 15. A method of making a heater for an aerospace component, the method comprising the steps of: A forming an electrical heating element on a layer of glass fibre reinforced thermoplastic film substrate; B applying two opposing thermoplastic layers on opposing sides of the intermediate layer; C applying heat and pressure to the layers to join the layers together; wherein the thermoplastic material contains an inorganic filler material. 16. A method as claimed in claim 15, wherein the thermoplastic material contains in excess of 20% of an inorganic filler material. 17. A method as claimed in claim 15 or 16, wherein the thermoplastic material is polyetheretherketone (PEEK) and the inorganic filler material is magnesium silicate. 18. A method as claimed in any of claims 15 to 17, wherein the thermoplastic layers are formed as a continuous process. 19. A method as claimed in claim 18, wherein a length of heater is cut into a predetermined length from the continuous process. 20. A method as claimed in any of claims 15 to 19 wherein the electrical heating element is formed on a thermoplastic substrate comprising glass fibre by a flame spraying process. 21 . A method as claimed in any of claims 15 to 20 comprising encapsulating the heating element in an intermediate thermoplastic layer. 22. A heater apparatus comprising an electrical operable heating element encapsulated in a layer of a thermoplastic, wherein the thermoplastic comprises in excess of 20% of an inorganic filler material. 23. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus comprising an inner layer of thermoplastic material and two opposing outer layers of thermoplastic material, the inner layer comprising an electrically operable heating element, wherein the inner layer of thermoplastic material contains an equal or lower percentage of an inorganic filling material than the two opposing outer layers. 24. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus as claimed in claim 23, wherein the thermoplastic is polyetheretherketone. 25. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus as claimed in claim 23, wherein the thermoplastic is polyaryletherketone (PAEK). 26. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus as claimed in claim 23, wherein the thermoplastic is polyetherketoneketone (PEKK). 27. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus as claimed in claim 23 or 26, wherein the inorganic filler material is magnesium silicate. 28. A multi-layer aero-surface heating apparatus as claimed in any of claims 23 to 27 wherein the inner layer comprises approximately 20%-30% of an inorganic filler and the outer layers comprise between 20% and 30% of the same inorganic filler material. 29. An aircraft aerodynamic surface comprising a heating apparatus according to any preceding claim. 30. An aircraft aerodynamic surface wherein the surface forms part of one of a wing, a nacelle, a rotor blade, a stabiliser or a tail. 31 . A multi-layer circuit board comprising an inner layer of thermoplastic material and two opposing outer layers of thermoplastic material, the inner layer comprising an electrical track forming an electrically operable heating element, wherein the inner layer of thermoplastic material contains an equal or lower percentage of an inorganic filling material than the two opposing outer layers.
